actionable_feedback: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_5', 'issue': 'Missing runnable notebook and CSV prevents reproducibility and independent checks.', 'fix': 'Provide a runnable open-source notebook (or .py script) that builds and solves both the reliability-constrained and naive cases and exports dispatch_schedule.csv with all computed flags.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Notebook/script runs end-to-end in a clean environment, produces dispatch_schedule.csv, and prints solver/log status deterministically.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_2', 'issue': 'Interconnection test appears to omit FR headroom in hours where FR is required.', 'fix': 'Enforce deliverability as: discharge_mw + regulation_mw + reserve_mw + fr_required_mw <= 105 whenever FR is required; otherwise exclude fr_required_mw.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Programmatic check confirms max(deliverable_export_mw) <= 105 with FR-adjusted constraint in all hours (tolerance interconnection_abs_mw=1e-6).'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_6', 'issue': 'Ramping explanation conflicts with table; unclear which signal is constrained.', 'fix': 'Define ramp on base net energy dispatch (P_base = discharge_mw - charge_mw) and enforce |P_base(t) - P_base(t-1)| <= 80 MW. Report ramp flags per hour.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'All ramp_ok flags true; report max observed ramp and the constrained signal definition in the memo.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_2', 'issue': 'Reliability flags not shown per hour (FR trigger, SCR reserve, post-contingency SOC).', 'fix': 'In CSV and memo tables, include columns and boolean flags: fr_required_mw, fr_ok, reserve_requirement_mw, reserve_ok, post_contingency_soc_mwh, pcsoc_ok, interconnection_ok, ramp_ok, soc_bounds_ok, throughput_ok, terminal_soc_ok.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'All flags true for the recommended schedule; violations enumerated for the naive case.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_4', 'issue': 'Objective decomposition missing; totals cannot be reconciled.', 'fix': 'Report per-hour economics: energy_revenue, regulation_revenue, reserve_revenue, charge_cost, degradation_cost (18 USD/MWh throughput), outage_risk_cost, reliability_penalty, and net_hourly. Sum to scenario totals.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Sum of per-hour components matches printed objective/net totals within 0.01 USD.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'Formulation/implementation not auditable.', 'fix': 'Enumerate all decision variables, constraints, and the full objective in the notebook, with assertions for SOC update, exclusivity (no simultaneous charge/discharge), bounds, throughput, FR/reserve triggers, and interconnection.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Code cells print model definitions; assertions pass; unit tests cover SOC balance and constraint activation logic.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_7', 'issue': 'Code clarity/testing not assessable.', 'fix': 'Refactor into documented functions with docstrings and add lightweight tests (e.g., pytest or inline asserts) for edge cases and numeric tolerances.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Tests run and pass; numeric tolerances documented and applied consistently.'}

{'rubric_item_id': 'ri_8', 'issue': 'Recommendation may be infeasible; limited sensitivity analysis.', 'fix': 'Revise the recommended stack to satisfy FR-adjusted interconnection; add brief sensitivity to regulation price and SCR conditions; document tie-breakers and robustness.', 'acceptance_criteria': 'Revised recommendation has all flags true with zero penalties; sensitivities summarized in memo.'}
artifact_specific_comments: {'artifact_ref': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comments': ['H3-H7 show dis+reg+res = 90 MW while FR headroom is listed as 25 MW; if FR counts toward interconnection, 115 > 105, implying infeasibility.', 'Ramping narrative cites 80 MW steps on H7->H8, but combined up-capacity appears unchanged (90->90); clarify the constrained signal (recommend base energy dispatch).', 'Per-hour economic components are not reported; only net_value_usd appears, preventing reconciliation.']}
feedback_summary: Only the memo artifact was delivered; the runnable notebook and CSV outputs are missing, preventing verification and reproducibility. The recommended schedule likely violates deliverability when FR headroom is required (hours H3-H7 appear to have dis+reg+res=90 MW while FR_required=25 MW against a 105 MW interconnection limit). Ramping explanations conflict with the tabulated values. Per-hour objective decomposition and reconciliation to the stated totals are not provided.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Notebook/script runs end-to-end in a clean environment, produces dispatch_schedule.csv, and prints solver/log status deterministically.
actionable_feedback.fix: Provide a runnable open-source notebook (or .py script) that builds and solves both the reliability-constrained and naive cases and exports dispatch_schedule.csv with all computed flags.
actionable_feedback.issue: Missing runnable notebook and CSV prevents reproducibility and independent checks.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Programmatic check confirms max(deliverable_export_mw) <= 105 with FR-adjusted constraint in all hours (tolerance interconnection_abs_mw=1e-6).
actionable_feedback.fix: Enforce deliverability as: discharge_mw + regulation_mw + reserve_mw + fr_required_mw <= 105 whenever FR is required; otherwise exclude fr_required_mw.
actionable_feedback.issue: Interconnection test appears to omit FR headroom in hours where FR is required.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: All ramp_ok flags true; report max observed ramp and the constrained signal definition in the memo.
actionable_feedback.fix: Define ramp on base net energy dispatch (P_base = discharge_mw - charge_mw) and enforce |P_base(t) - P_base(t-1)| <= 80 MW. Report ramp flags per hour.
actionable_feedback.issue: Ramping explanation conflicts with table; unclear which signal is constrained.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: All flags true for the recommended schedule; violations enumerated for the naive case.
actionable_feedback.fix: In CSV and memo tables, include columns and boolean flags: fr_required_mw, fr_ok, reserve_requirement_mw, reserve_ok, post_contingency_soc_mwh, pcsoc_ok, interconnection_ok, ramp_ok, soc_bounds_ok, throughput_ok, terminal_soc_ok.
actionable_feedback.issue: Reliability flags not shown per hour (FR trigger, SCR reserve, post-contingency SOC).

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Sum of per-hour components matches printed objective/net totals within 0.01 USD.
actionable_feedback.fix: Report per-hour economics: energy_revenue, regulation_revenue, reserve_revenue, charge_cost, degradation_cost (18 USD/MWh throughput), outage_risk_cost, reliability_penalty, and net_hourly. Sum to scenario totals.
actionable_feedback.issue: Objective decomposition missing; totals cannot be reconciled.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Code cells print model definitions; assertions pass; unit tests cover SOC balance and constraint activation logic.
actionable_feedback.fix: Enumerate all decision variables, constraints, and the full objective in the notebook, with assertions for SOC update, exclusivity (no simultaneous charge/discharge), bounds, throughput, FR/reserve triggers, and interconnection.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Tests run and pass; numeric tolerances documented and applied consistently.
actionable_feedback.fix: Refactor into documented functions with docstrings and add lightweight tests (e.g., pytest or inline asserts) for edge cases and numeric tolerances.

actionable_feedback.acceptance_criteria: Revised recommendation has all flags true with zero penalties; sensitivities summarized in memo.
actionable_feedback.fix: Revise the recommended stack to satisfy FR-adjusted interconnection; add brief sensitivity to regulation price and SCR conditions; document tie-breakers and robustness.
actionable_feedback.issue: Recommendation may be infeasible; limited sensitivity analysis.

comments: H3-H7 show dis+reg+res = 90 MW while FR headroom is listed as 25 MW; if FR counts toward interconnection, 115 > 105, implying infeasibility.
Ramping narrative cites 80 MW steps on H7->H8, but combined up-capacity appears unchanged (90->90); clarify the constrained signal (recommend base energy dispatch).
Per-hour economic components are not reported; only net_value_usd appears, preventing reconciliation.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Deliver a runnable optimization notebook/script and dispatch_schedule.csv. Correct the interconnection constraint to include FR headroom when FR is required, compute ramp on base energy dispatch, and add per-hour reliability flags and economic components. Re-optimize, update the memo tables/figures, and ensure all checks pass with zero penalties for the recommended schedule. Include objective reconciliation and minimal tests.
